mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis instances on Kubernetes, supporting standalone, cluster, and sentinel modes. It provides built-in monitoring and adheres to best practices for security and performance. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redis Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://opstree.github.io/redis-operator

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for the Redis Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Redis Operator in the 'redis-operator' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redis-operator || true

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.24.0

```

> Creates the namespace if it doesn't already exist.
> Installs the Redis Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redis Operator deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> You should see the Redis Operator pod running.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Redis Cluster configuration to deploy a Redis cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Redis cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redis cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator

```

> You should see your Redis cluster listed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Be cautious as this will delete all Redis instances managed by the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis-operator

kubectl delete namespace redis-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Redis Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Redis data. This step will upgrade the Redis Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.24.0

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Backup existing Redis configurations.
> Upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Redis Operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Redis cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis Operator may not have sufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the operator has the necessary RBAC permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis pod may be misconfigured or unable to start due to resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redis-operator' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Redis Custom Resource configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis Operator may not be installed or the release name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation with 'helm list --namespace redis-operator'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis 7.x, improved monitoring capabilities with redis-exporter.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.19 or earlier, review the new configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redis cluster listed with the command 'kubectl get redis -n redis-operator' and the pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n redis-operator'.

metadata:

  replicas: 3
EOF
